46.reason: Variable i is out of scope here; i is from the previous loop and may be dot, causing out-of-bounds access

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Variable i is out of scope here; i is the loop variable from previous for loop, but after that loop i equals dot, so this writes beyond array bounds
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Variable i is out of scope here; i is from the previous loop and may be dot, causing out-of-bounds access
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  error: uses index i (value after previous loop) instead of v, leading to out-of-bounds access
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  variable i is the number of vertices (out of bounds); should be G[v][j]=9999
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Incorrect row index 'i', should be 'v' to clear the adjacency list of the deleted vertex.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Logical error: 'i' holds the value 'dot' from the previous loop. Should be 'G[v][j] = 9999;'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop variable 'i' is used outside its intended scope; after the previous loop, 'i' equals 'dot', so G[i][j] accesses out-of-bounds memory. Should be G[v][j] = 9999;]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop variable 'i' is used after loop ends; 'i' equals 'dot' here, so writing to G[dot][j] is out-of-bounds]
